rent.note: furnished 1BR in nomad area (Ponsonby, Newmarket, Grey Lynn); city center NZD 2,600/mo (~$1,560); outer suburbs 20-30% less

utilities.note: electric + water + internet; NZ energy costs moderate; internet ~NZD 85/mo

groceries.note: home cooking; Countdown/Pak'nSave/New World; NZD 400-600/mo ($240-360); mid estimate

diningOut.note: Auckland dining pricey; lunch ~NZD 18-25, dinner NZD 30-50; excellent café scene

transit.note: AT HOP monthly ~NZD 170 (~$100); transit less comprehensive than AU/EU cities

coworking.note: hot-desk monthly; Auckland coworking ~NZD 350-450/mo; good selection in CBD

misc.note: gym (~NZD 50), toiletries, entertainment, outdoor recreation, beach trips buffer

accomSeasonality._doc: 12 monthly multipliers for RENT ONLY (Jan..Dec). 1.0 = base. Auckland is Southern Hemisphere — summer Dec-Feb. Medium-term furnished lets fairly stable.

evidence.quote: digital nomads should plan for approximately $4,261/month in Auckland, covering coworking, accommodation, food, and local transport
Rent averages from NZD $2,600 for a one-bedroom apartment in the city centre, though outer suburbs like Takapuna, Henderson, and Manukau offer more affordable options with rents 20–30% lower
Groceries average NZD $600, though groceries for a single person can range from NZD 400–600 per month
